_description: Hesburger (fast food, Bulgaria), 17-07-2026. Dual EUR/BGN receipt with NO labelled SUBTOTAL line — only grand totals: 'ОБЩА СУМА ЕВРО' 12.93 (euro) and 'ОБЩА СУМА ЛЕВА' 25.29 (leva @ 1.95583). The FIRST grand total printed is '#ОБЩА СУМА В ЛЕВ(А) 25.29' (leva), so the model previously wrote 25.29 into `total` while the line items are in EUR (11.04 + 1.89 = 12.93) — nearly DOUBLING the recorded spend. The client couldn't catch it: items-sum < total is the benign deposit/fee direction (soft over-read notice and the validateExtraction overshoot guard only fire the other way), there is no printed SUBTOTAL to anchor the blocking shortfall dialog, and <3 items disables the article-count guard — so the 12.36 gap was silently absorbed as extraCharges. Correct behaviour: `total` = the EURO grand total 12.93; the BGN 25.29 must be ignored.
_image: Drop the receipt photo here as receipt.jpg (gitignored — it's personal data).
_crux: total must be 12.93 (EUR), never 25.29 (BGN). This case fails without the dual-currency clause in receiptPrompt.js.
_taxNote: VAT-inclusive: the 'Б' after each price is the fiscal VAT group, not a tax-on-top charge, so tax = 0.
